workflow.name: Extract client meeting insights from Google Sheets with Groq and Gmail
workflow.description:
# Client Meeting Notes → AI Extraction using n8n, OpenAI & Google Sheets

This workflow automatically processes meeting notes stored in Google Sheets, extracts key insights like action items, follow-ups and risks using AI and stores the results in an output sheet. It also sends a summary email and marks records as processed to avoid duplication.


### Quick Start Guide

1. [Login to your n8n account](https://n8n.partnerlinks.io/om1efg2qgvwi)
2. Connect Google Sheets (input + output)
3. Add OpenAI or Groq credentials
4. Ensure "Processed" column exists
5. Configure email node
6. Run workflow


## What It Does

This workflow automates the entire lifecycle of meeting note processing. It fetches raw notes from Google Sheets, filters only unprocessed entries and prepares them for AI analysis.

Using an AI model, it extracts structured insights such as action items, follow-ups and risks. These insights are then formatted into readable text and stored in a separate output sheet.

Finally, the workflow updates the original record as processed and sends a summary email, ensuring efficient communication and eliminating manual effort.


## Who It's For

- Project Managers  
- Business Analysts  
- Operations Teams  
- Startup Founders  
- Anyone handling frequent meetings  


## Requirements

- [n8n account](https://n8n.partnerlinks.io/om1efg2qgvwi)
- Google Sheets account  
- OpenAI or Groq API key  
- Gmail account (for email notifications)  


## How It Works & Setup Guide

### Step 1: Trigger

Manual trigger starts the workflow.

### Step 2: Fetch Data

Google Sheets node retrieves meeting notes.

### Step 3: Filter Records

IF node checks if "Processed" field is empty.

### Step 4: Prepare Data

Set node formats notes into `raw_notes`.

### Step 5: AI Extraction

AI Agent extracts:

- Action Items
- Follow-ups
- Risks

### Step 6: Format Output

Code node converts JSON into readable text.

### Step 7: Mark Processed

Updates original sheet with "Yes".

### Step 8: Store Results

Appends extracted data into output sheet.

### Step 9: Send Email

Gmail node sends summary email.


## Customize Nodes

- Modify AI prompt to extract more fields  
- Change email format/content  
- Add additional fields in output sheet  
- Adjust filtering logic  


## Add-ons

- Slack/Teams notifications  
- Auto-trigger using webhook  
- Dashboard integration  
- Priority tagging  


## Use Cases

1. Client meeting tracking  
2. Internal team updates  
3. Sales call summaries  
4. Project review documentation  
5. Risk tracking systems  

More use cases can be built using this workflow.


## Troubleshooting

| Issue | Cause | Solution |
|------|------|---------|
| No data processed | "Processed" already filled | Clear field |
| AI output invalid | Wrong prompt | Fix JSON format |
| Email not sent | Gmail auth issue | Reconnect account |
| Data not stored | Mapping error | Check fields |


## Need Help?

If you need help setting up or customizing this workflow, feel free to reach out to our [n8n developers](https://www.weblineindia.com/hire-n8n-developers/) at [WeblineIndia for expert assistance](https://www.weblineindia.com/contact-us.html).

parameters.content:
# Client Meeting Notes → Action Items Automation
# How It Works

This workflow automates the processing of meeting notes from Google Sheets by identifying and handling only unprocessed records. It prepares the notes and sends them to an AI model, which extracts key insights such as action items, follow-ups and risks in a structured format. The extracted data is then formatted for readability and stored in an output sheet for tracking.

Finally, the workflow marks the original record as processed to avoid duplication and sends a summary email containing all extracted details to the user. This ensures efficient meeting follow-up, clear task tracking and quick communication without manual effort.

# Setup Steps

## 1. Connect Google Sheets
Authenticate input and output sheets

## 2. Configure Processing Logic
Ensure only unprocessed rows are handled

## 3. Configure AI Model
Connect OpenAI/Groq and set extraction prompt

## 4. Configure Output Storage
Map extracted fields to output sheet

## 5. Set Email Notification
Add recipient and email format

## 6. Test Workflow
Run and verify extraction and email output

## Input & Data Fetch

Fetches meeting notes from Google Sheets and triggers the workflow. Ensures only unprocessed records are considered for automation.

## Data Preparation

Prepares and structures raw meeting notes and user details for AI processing, ensuring clean and usable input data.

## AI Processing, Extraction & Data Transformation

Uses AI to extract action items, follow-ups and risks from meeting notes in structured JSON format. The output is then parsed and formatted into readable text fields for storage and reporting.

## Data Storage & Notification

Stores extracted action items, follow-ups and risks in the output sheet, marks records as processed to avoid duplication and sends a summary email for quick review and follow-up actions.

parameters.text:
=You are a meeting assistant.

Extract:

- Action Items
- Follow-ups
- Risks

Meeting Notes:
{{$json["raw_notes"]}}

IMPORTANT:
- Return ONLY valid JSON
- Do NOT add quotes around JSON
- Do NOT add \n or formatting
- Do NOT add any explanation

Return exactly like this:

{
  "action_items": ["item1", "item2"],
  "follow_ups": ["item1"],
  "risks": ["item1"]
}

parameters.subject: Meeting Summary

json.Notes: Client wants faster delivery, budget is tight, follow up next week

The client discussed the ongoing project and emphasized the need to significantly improve delivery timelines, as the current pace is not meeting their expectations. They also mentioned that the design team needs to coordinate better with the development team to avoid delays and miscommunication.

There was a concern raised about the project budget, as it is already close to the allocated limit. Any additional changes or delays may result in exceeding the budget. The client is also unsure about the scalability of the current solution and whether it can handle future growth.

The team agreed to review the current workflow and identify bottlenecks that are slowing down the process. It was suggested that weekly progress updates should be shared with the client to maintain transparency.

Additionally, the client requested a demo of the updated product features by next week and asked for documentation to be prepared for all new changes. There is also a dependency on a third-party API which has been unstable recently, posing a risk to the project timeline.

Overall, the focus should be on faster delivery, better coordination and staying within budget while ensuring quality.

The client discussed the ongoing project and emphasized the need to significantly improve delivery timelines, as the current pace is not meeting their expectations. They also mentioned that the design team needs to coordinate better with the development team to avoid delays and miscommunication.

There was a concern raised about the project budget, as it is already close to the allocated limit. Any additional changes or delays may result in exceeding the budget. The client is also unsure about the scalability of the current solution and whether it can handle future growth.

The team agreed to review the current workflow and identify bottlenecks that are slowing down the process. It was suggested that weekly progress updates should be shared with the client to maintain transparency.

Additionally, the client requested a demo of the updated product features by next week and asked for documentation to be prepared for all new changes. There is also a dependency on a third-party API which has been unstable recently, posing a risk to the project timeline.

Overall, the focus should be on faster delivery, better coordination and staying within budget while ensuring quality.

json.output: {"action_items":["Review workflow and identify bottlenecks","Share weekly progress updates with client","Prepare demo of updated product features by next week","Prepare documentation for all new changes","Improve coordination between design and development teams","Mitigate third‑party API instability","Monitor and control project budget","Assess and ensure solution scalability"],"follow_ups":["Demo delivery confirmation with client","Weekly progress update schedule","Budget status review","Scalability assessment","Third‑party API stability check"],"risks":["Third‑party API instability affecting timeline","Potential budget overrun due to changes/delays","Uncertainty about solution scalability for future growth","Delays caused by poor design‑development coordination"]}

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
